title: Buttery Saudi Arabian Lamb Skillet
description: A rich, buttery lamb skillet infused with Middle Eastern spices and a splash of sake, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium‑high heat and add butter and olive oil until the butter melts.
Add the lamb cubes and sear until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and spices (cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cayenne) and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the sake to deglaze the pan, scraping up any browned bits; let the liquid reduce for 2 minutes.
Return the lamb to the skillet, pour in the broth, bring to a gentle simmer, cover, and cook for 15 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice, season with salt and pepper to taste.
Finish the dish by sprinkling chopped parsley on top. Serve hot over rice or with flatbread.
